headline: World Bank WARNS Gold and Silver Already Priced for Peak Risk — Sees Ceiling Through 2026
snippet: The World Bank projects a price ceiling for gold and silver through 2026, arguing peak geopolitical risk is already fully priced in and that rate-driven demand destruction will cap further upside — a contrarian institutional note even as central bank accumulation remains near record levels.

headline: EVERYONE AGREES IT'S A CRISIS. GOLD STILL DECLINES. — Fibonacci Signal Flashes Deeper Drop Ahead
snippet: A 0.21% USD Index move triggered a 2%+ gold decline — about 10x the dollar's move — as gold fell to its 38.2% Fibonacci retracement. The analysis argues gold 'wants' to decline significantly before turning back up, warning retail investors that geopolitical crisis alone is not sufficient to hold this level.

headline: SAGA METALS HITS 55 FROM 55: 107m at 53% Fe, 6.5% Ti, 0.44% V at Labrador Critical Minerals Project
snippet: SAGA Metals (TSXV: SAGA) reported its latest maiden MRE drill results at the Radar Titanium-Vanadium-Iron project in Labrador — holes R-0027 through R-0029 returned 80m, 106m, and 107m of oxide mineralization, maintaining a perfect 55-from-55 hit rate and building toward a resource estimate for these battery and industrial critical minerals.

headline: NICOLA MINING Drills Into Jotun Porphyry Target at New Craigmont — BC Copper Project Sits Next to Highland Valley
snippet: Nicola Mining (TSXV: NIM) commenced its $1.5M 2026 diamond drill program at the New Craigmont copper project near Merritt, BC, targeting the Jotun porphyry copper zone on a 10,800-hectare property directly adjacent to Teck's Highland Valley Copper — Canada's largest copper mine.

headline: UZBEKISTAN'S QIZILKOK Uranium Mine Goes Commercial — Navoiyuran's ISR Cuts Production Costs 2–3x
snippet: Navoiyuran has started commercial uranium production at the 10,900-tonne-U Qizilkok deposit in Uzbekistan using in-situ recovery (ISR) oxygen technology the company says reduces production costs by 2–3x versus conventional methods, adding non-Kazakh supply to a market where spot uranium holds above $100/lb.

headline: WORLD'S BIGGEST LITHIUM MINE Slashes Output on 'Systemic Issues' — IGO Cuts Greenbushes Guidance 10%
snippet: IGO Ltd. cut full-year production guidance at Greenbushes — the world's largest hard-rock lithium mine in Western Australia — from 1.50–1.65 million tonnes down to 1.38–1.43 million tonnes, citing 'systemic issues' at the site and tightening near-term supply in a market that has rebounded 125% from its 2025 trough.

headline: REPORT: U.S. GOVERNMENT SECRETLY BUYING CARTEL GOLD — Money Metals Flags Anomalous Treasury Holdings
snippet: Money Metals Exchange reports on claims the U.S. government has been covertly acquiring gold from sources tied to Latin American cartels, with analysts arguing the purchases explain anomalous Treasury holdings data. If the claims hold, it implies a structural government-level bid under the gold price.

headline: SILVER DEFICIT ENTERS YEAR SIX: China Already Imported 790+ Tonnes in 2026 — BofA Bull Case: $309/oz
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT analysis confirms silver is entering its sixth consecutive structural deficit year, with China alone importing over 790 tonnes in early 2026 — driven largely by solar panel manufacturing. Bank of America's bull case places silver between $135 and $309/oz by year-end.

headline: BUY AGNICO EAGLE OR ENDEAVOUR SILVER in April? Motley Fool Canada Makes the Bull Case at $4,900 Gold
snippet: With gold projected to average $4,906/oz in 2026 and Agnico Eagle delivering US$8.8B EBITDA and US$4.4B free cash flow in 2025, Motley Fool Canada argues both AEM and Endeavour Silver (EDR) remain undervalued against the metal — and offer leverage to any further precious metals rally.
